levels.title: First Word, Last Lie
levels.prompt: Type the first word in this prompt, not the shiny word LAST.

failure: LAST glittered and your finger bought the whole scam.
You chased the loud word. Tiny hot breath journalism.
delay: The first word is standing at the front like it paid rent.
scorecard: You found word one. The sentence is filing a formal complaint.

levels.uqiq_moment: The answer is the literal first word of the prompt while LAST tries to steal the room.

levels.title: NO Triggers YES
levels.prompt: If this sentence contains the word NO, type YES.

failure: NO waved once and your smooth brain joined the parade.
The condition was true. Your answer was furniture.
delay: Find NO, then do what the sentence says before it gets smug.
scorecard: You converted NO into YES. Philosophy is now lightly annoyed.

levels.uqiq_moment: The player must answer YES because the prompt does contain NO.

levels.title: Banana Embargo

failure: You obeyed the lying sentence. Elite hall monitor energy.
The forbidden word sat there and you treated it like a museum rope.
delay: The Level has one word with a security guard. That is probably the word.
scorecard: You typed the banned fruit. Compliance has left the chat.

levels.uqiq_moment: The only way to win is to type the word the prompt says not to type.

levels.title: Punctuation Witness
levels.prompt: This sentence is not asking anything? Type the punctuation that disagrees.

failure: The question mark testified and you cross-examined a brick.
Punctuation solved it. You arrived as decorative luggage.
delay: The tiny hook at the end is snitching with perfect posture.
scorecard: You trusted punctuation over words. Growth, in a very small hat.

levels.uqiq_moment: The sentence claims it is not a question while ending with a question mark.

levels.title: Label Perjury
levels.prompt: Tap the button whose label is lying.

failure: You believed a button under oath. Courtroom dingleberry behavior.
The honest-looking label mugged your attention in broad daylight.
delay: One button says it is wrong. If it wins, that label is the liar.
scorecard: You caught a rectangle committing perjury. The law remains tiny.

levels.uqiq_moment: The correct button is labeled I AM WRONG, making its label the lie.

levels.title: Word Count Problem
levels.prompt: Type the number of words after the colon: one two three.

solution.explanation: Only the words after the colon count, so one two three makes the answer 3.

failure: You argued with the colon and lost to three tiny words.
The count was three. Your confidence filed the wrong invoice.
delay: Only count after the colon. The words before it are lobby noise.
scorecard: You counted to three without adopting the whole sentence. Historic restraint.

levels.uqiq_moment: The colon is the boundary: only one two three count, so the answer is 3.

levels.title: Word-Only Intruder
levels.prompt: Find the cell that breaks the word-only pattern.

solution.explanation: 404 is the only numbered cell in a grid of word labels.

failure: You selected a word in the word-only trap. Spectacularly decorative.
The number wore a siren and you interviewed the alphabet.
delay: Eight cells are words. One cell showed up as math paperwork.
scorecard: You found the number pretending it belonged. The grid is embarrassed.

levels.uqiq_moment: A number sits in the middle of a grid where every other cell is a word.

levels.title: Word Before Lie
levels.prompt: Type the word before LIE: truth LIE.

failure: LIE shouted and you forgot the word standing directly before it.
Your attention got pickpocketed by three capital letters.
delay: Look immediately left of LIE. This is not a treasure map.
scorecard: You found truth before lie. The theme is coping poorly.

levels.uqiq_moment: The loud word LIE is only a locator; the answer is the quieter word before it.

levels.title: Flash of False Confidence
levels.prompt: Flash the words, then choose the order they actually appeared.

rules.scene_prompt: Tap tiles into the recall slots in the flashed order.

solution.explanation: Recall the original flash order instead of the reversed choice order.

failure: The words shuffled once and your memory fell into a folding chair.
You remembered the button row, not the flash. Premium wrong receipt.
delay: Flash first, hide second, press the original order before confidence spoils.
scorecard: You held three dishonest words in order. Tiny miracle, loud paperwork.

levels.uqiq_moment: The choice order is reversed, so trusting the visible buttons gives the wrong sequence.

levels.title: Final Answer Is Not Final
levels.prompt: Type the final word of this prompt: decoy answer.

failure: DECOY put on a tiny crown and you pledged loyalty.
The final word was last in line. You still tackled the wrong guest.
delay: Read all the way to the end. The last word is doing zero cardio.
scorecard: You finished Pack 2 by reading the end. Literature has peaked.

levels.uqiq_moment: The final word is answer, while decoy exists only to pull the player's eyes off target.
